judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent, hitting all four expectations with comparable evidence and grounding; report 2 is slightly more thorough/organized but neither is clearly superior in substance.

report_1.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Remove or rewrite as opt-in... **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — not implemented.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `/visits` list endpoint (returning `[]`) and a `# TODO: pdf export` comment; there is no export route... Remove this section.

report_2.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default"** — contradicted by the code. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py:4` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`... **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF"** — not implemented. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `/visits` list endpoint and the comment `# TODO: pdf export` (line 11)... Remove the section.

report_1.evidence: The "after" image is not the scheduling calendar at all. `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, and the image itself literally shows "BILLING / INVOICES" while the "before" shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR". Customers would be shown a billing screen as the "redesigned calendar."

report_2.evidence: `docs/release/img/before_scheduling.png` shows the scheduling calendar ("SCHEDULING CALENDAR"), but `docs/release/img/after_scheduling.png` shows a billing screen ("BILLING / INVOICES"). The manifest confirms this isn't just a labeling accident: `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`.

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` is referenced by the doc but does not exist** — the only image files are `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, and `unused_capture_07.png`. The manifest confirms: "expected capture; no corresponding file."... **`unused_capture_07.png` ("PATIENT LIST") exists in the release img folder but is referenced nowhere** — orphaned; it should be removed

report_2.evidence: `after_export.png` — **referenced by the doc but does not exist on disk**; `captures.json` itself notes "expected capture; no corresponding file"... `unused_capture_07.png` — **exists on disk (shows "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced nowhere** in the doc or manifest; orphaned capture that should be removed or wired up deliberately.

report_1.evidence: The trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** is an internal drafting note (and the word "draft" itself) — it should not go to customers.

report_2.evidence: The footer **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** is an attribution/process artifact, and the word "draft" itself flags the document as unfinished... it should not go to customers as-is; confirm the owner's policy and either remove the line or rephrase, and definitely drop "draft".
